item.title: Retired Lt Gen Masud shown arrested, to be interrogated today
item.description: The International Crimes Tribunal-2 on Thursday allowed investigators to interrogate former Jatiya Party lawmaker and retired Lieutenant General Masud Uddin Chowdhury for one day in a crimes against humanity case linked to the July uprising of 2024....

item.description: A Dhaka court on Thursday framed charges against three people including former minister and veteran Awami League leader Tofail Ahmed in a case lodged for allegedly embezzling Tk 1.25 crore by misusing power....

item.description: Today is the 165th anniversary of birth of Nobel laureate Rabindranath Tagore, a key and influential figure in Bangla literature....Country specific content

item.title: BB extends support to distressed businesses
item.description: Bangladesh Bank has extended the deadline for special loan rescheduling facilities for distressed businesses until June 30, allowing borrowers to regularise defaulted loans with nominal down payments under a relaxed policy support scheme......

item.title: Forex rules for transport services unified
item.description: Bangladesh Bank has issued new consolidated guidelines to make foreign exchange transactions in international travel, airlines, shipping, freight forwarding, tour operations and overseas transport services more transparent, regulated and easier......

item.description: Three-day ‘Dhaka Industrial Packaging Expo 2026’ began on Thursday at Bangladesh-China Friendship Conference Centre at Agargaon in the capital, said a press release......

item.description: West Bengal chief minister Mamata Banerjee’s last gambit — refusal to resign her post — ended in an anti-climax of sorts as governor RN Ravi dissolved the state’s legislative assembly, reports NDTV....

item.title: Solar power policy on cards, says minister
item.description: Power, energy and mineral resources minister Iqbal Hassan Mahmood on Thursday said that the government was working to quickly formulate a policy for the solar and renewable energy sector......

item.description: The state run Eastern Refinery PLC will start operation activities of its main unit from early Friday as imported crude oil is being receiving at its storage tanks, official said......

item.description: Oil prices fell and global stock markets traded mixed Thursday awaiting an update on a US plan to end the Middle East war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz......

item.description: The Bangladesh Nationalist Party is facing mounting organisational challenges as 10 of its 11 affiliated and associate bodies are operating with expired committees....

item.description: A WAVE of renewed optimism is sweeping across Meherpur’s farming communities as cotton cultivation expands sharply this season, driven by fair market prices, improved yields, and rising farmer confidence......

item.description: The price of gold in Bangladesh has increased again, with the rate of 22-carat gold rising by Tk 2,215 per bhari following a fresh hike in the price of pure gold in the local market......

item.title: Australia to force gas giants to reserve fuel for domestic use
item.description: Australia will look to stave off energy shortages by forcing major gas companies to reserve 20 per cent of their exports for domestic use, energy minister Chris Bowen said Thursday......

item.description: The United States was waiting on Thursday for Iran to respond to its latest proposed deal to end the war in the Middle East and to reopen the key shipping lane out of the Gulf....

item.title: German factory orders surge
item.description: German factory orders rose unexpectedly strongly in March, official data showed Thursday, boosted by firms rushing to get orders in to beat disruption provoked by the Mideast war......

item.title: Musk’s SpaceX strikes data centre deal with Anthropic
item.description: AI startup Anthropic announced Wednesday it has agreed to a major computing partnership with Elon Musk’s SpaceX, securing access to a vast data centre as the Claude maker scrambles to keep pace with surging demand for its AI services......

item.title: Shell quarterly net profit jumps 19pc as war fuels oil prices
item.description: British energy giant Shell announced Thursday a 19-per cent increase in net profits for the first quarter as the Middle East war sent oil and gas prices soaring in volatile trading......

item.title: Many students end pry edn without basic skills
item.description: Many students in Bangladesh enter the secondary school without foundational literacy and numeracy skills, revealed a report of the United Nations Children’s Fund on Thursday. ...

item.title: AirAsia signs $19b deal for 150 Airbus A220 jets
item.description: Southeast Asia’s AirAsia signed a $19 billion deal to buy 150 Canadian-made Airbus A220-300 jets, the two companies said, with the low-cost carrier adding Thursday that it could double the order to meet future demand......

item.description: As part of Premier Bank PLC’s ‘Deposit and Recovery Campaign 2026,’ the bank has achieved a significant milestone of Tk 1,144 crore in fresh deposits within 26 working days since April 1, said a press release.....

item.title: Will lawmakers discuss LDC graduation?
item.description: BANGLADESH’S new parliament has become noticeably more vibrant, with debates and discussions, ranging from substantive policy issues to more routine and even trivial matters finding increasing space in the House. This renewed energy is welcome......Global headlines service

item.title: Too little, too late for Rohingya adolescents
item.description: SYRIAN refugee Yusra Mardini, for example, not only competed for the Refugee Olympic team after fleeing war but also became the youngest goodwill ambassador for the UNHCR. Boxer Cindy Ngamba, who fled Cameroon as a child, made history by winning the first Olympic medal for the Refugee Olympic team in Paris in 2024. Muzoon Almellehan, too, rose from camp......

item.title: Silent ovarian cancer crisis
item.description: BANGLADESH has made important progress in expanding healthcare access over the past decades, yet ovarian cancer remains one of the country’s most overlooked and under-discussed public health challenges. Unlike breast or cervical cancer, ovarian cancer receives little public attention, limited policy focus and minimal awareness investment despite its high mortality......

item.title: Uneven development
item.description: BANGLADESH’S development story is usually told through a familiar set of statistics — steady gross domestic product growth, the rise of the ready-made garments sector, declining poverty rates and improvements in human development indicators. These achievements are real and they matter. Yet they reveal only part of the country’s reality. Development, in any......

item.title: Saving rivers requires more than rhetoric
item.description: THE unabated discharge of untreated industrial effluents into rivers and water bodies in blatant violation of laws and repeated court directives reflects a grave criminal negligence on part of industries and the authorities. A photograph that New Age published on May 7 shows untreated industrial waste discharged directly into the River Buriganga on the outskirts of Dhaka. Such......Health and wellness

item.title: Govt should shield the poor from inflation
item.description: THE Bureau of Statistics reports an increase in general inflation, which increased to 9.04 per cent in April after it had eased to 8.71 per cent in March. The easing of inflation in March happened after an upward trend that had continued for five months since October 2025. The national statistical office says that food inflation increased in April to 8.39 per cent, up from......

item.description: Speakers at an event on Thursday said that uncontrolled hill cutting, unplanned urbanisation and large-scale deforestation are the primary human-induced factors aggravating landslide risks in Chattogram and Bandarban districts....

item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Thursday directed officials to prepare a transparent and accurate list of farmers affected by heavy rainfall in the haor region to ensure swift financial assistance....

item.description: The electricity and gas connections to 20 industrial factories operating without effluent treatment plants along the banks of the Sitalakhya River in Narayanganj have been disconnected following a High Court order....

item.description: One hundred and seventy four more Bangladeshi nationals returned home from Libya early Thursday under a voluntary repatriation programme, implemented jointly by the government, the Bangladesh mission in Libya and the International Organization for Migration....Maps

item.description: The cabinet on Thursday approved a seven-day holiday for the forthcoming Eid-ul-Azha, declaring public holidays for May 25–31....

item.description: National Citizen Party convener and lawmaker Nahid Islam on Thursday urged the government to strengthen treatment facilities to prevent further child deaths....

item.description: A court in Dhaka on Thursday ordered the seizure of tax records belonging to former Jahangirnagar University vice chancellor professor Farzana Islam and her husband as part of an anti-corruption investigation into alleged recruitment trade and illicit wealth accumulation....

item.description: Information and broadcasting minister Zahir Uddin Swapon on Thursday said the current government does not want to control the media, but aims to establish it within a fair and independent framework....Country specific content

item.description: Leaders of a faction of Jatiya Party on Thursday urged the Indian government for signing the Teesta river water sharing deal with Bangladesh and resuming the issuance of visas for Bangladeshi tourists who want to visit India....

item.description: A motorcyclist was killed in a road accident by a pickup van near Sultana Kamal Bridge area under Demra police station in Dhaka Thursday morning....

item.description: The cabinet committee on government purchase at a meeting on Thursday approved the procurement of three cargoes of liquefied natural gas, two crore litres of soya bean oil and 163 vehicles for the Rapid Action Battalion....

item.description: Russia’s defence ministry said on Thursday that its forces had destroyed almost 350 Ukrainian drones overnight, days before Moscow holds World War II commemorations and amid competing calls from both sides for a temporary ceasefire....

item.description: Prime minister Narendra Modi said India remains ‘steadfast as ever’ in its determination to defeat terrorism and its ‘enabling ecosystem’, marking one year since a deadly clash with arch-enemy Pakistan....

item.description: The Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government on Thursday decided to provide Family Cards to 41 lakh beneficiaries at a cost of around Tk 12,373 crore under the social safety net programme in the next financial year 2026–27....

item.description: The World Health Organisation said Thursday that more cases of the hantavirus could emerge but expected the outbreak to be ‘limited’ if precautions are taken, after the disease killed three passengers from a cruise ship....

item.description: Six people, including two children, were killed when mortar shells hit a market in a turbulent area of northwestern Pakistan, a police official said on Thursday....
